content:
# 1 Timothy Chapter 6 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1TI_006_page_001.jpg, 1TI_006_page_002.jpg

1. Let all who are under a yoke as bondservants regard their own masters as worthy of all honor, so that the name of God and the teaching may not be reviled.

2. Those who have believing masters must not be disrespectful on the ground that they are brothers; rather they must serve all the more, since those who benefit by their good service are believers and beloved. Teach and urge these things.

3. If anyone teaches a different doctrine and does not agree with the sound words of our Lord Jesus Christ and the teaching that accords with godliness,

4. he is puffed up with conceit and understands nothing. He has an unhealthy craving for controversy and for quarrels about words, which produce envy, dissension, slander, evil suspicions,

5. and constant friction among people who are depraved in mind and deprived of the truth, imagining that godliness is a means of gain.

6. But godliness with contentment is great gain,

7. for we brought nothing into the world, and we cannot take anything out of the world.

8. But if we have food and clothing, with these we will be content.

9. But those who desire to be rich fall into temptation, into a snare, into many senseless and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction.

10. For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evils. It is through this craving that some have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves with many pangs.

11. But as for you, O man of God, flee these things. Pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, steadfastness, gentleness.

12. Fight the good fight of the faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called and about which you made the good confession in the presence of many witnesses.

13. I charge you in the presence of God, who gives life to all things, and of Christ Jesus, who in his testimony before Pontius Pilate made the good confession,

14. to keep the commandment unstained and free from reproach until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ,

15. which he will display at the proper time — he who is the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords,

16. who alone has immortality, who dwells in unapproachable light, whom no one has ever seen or can see. To him be honor and eternal dominion. Amen.

17. As for the rich in this present age, charge them not to be haughty, nor to set their hopes on the uncertainty of riches, but on God, who richly provides us with everything to enjoy.

18. They are to do good, to be rich in good works, to be generous and ready to share,

19. thus storing up treasure for themselves as a good foundation for the future, so that they may take hold of that which is truly life.

20. O Timothy, guard the deposit entrusted to you. Avoid the irreverent babble and contradictions of what is falsely called "knowledge,"

21. for by professing it some have swerved from the faith. Grace be with you.

---
## SOPHIA's Council Notes

Chapter 6 addresses slaves and masters (vv.1-2), false teachers and money (vv.3-10), Timothy's charge (vv.11-16), instructions to the rich (vv.17-19), and the final appeal (vv.20-21). The money discourse (vv.6-10) and the sovereign doxology (vv.15-16) are the chapter's theological peaks.

**Verses 6-10 — Contentment theology:** All three translators captured the logic. SOPHIA affirms: Paul's teaching is not anti-wealth but anti-craving. The desire to be rich, not riches themselves, is the trap.

**Verse 20 — "Falsely called knowledge":** All three translators preserved the anti-gnōsis warning. SOPHIA affirms: this may be the earliest NT polemic against proto-Gnostic claims.

---
## ⚠️ Disagreement Log

### v.1 — "slaves under the yoke" vs. "All slaves"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "Let as many as are slaves under [the] yoke" | hosoi eisin hypo zygon douloi |
| LOGOS | "Let all who are under a yoke as bondservants" | Same — "yoke" preserved |
| PNEUMA | "All slaves should show full respect for their masters" | Drops "under the yoke" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. "Under the yoke" (hypo zygon) specifies a particular condition — not all enslaved people, but those under the heavy burden of slavery. PNEUMA drops the phrase.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA drops "under the yoke."

---

### v.5 — "imagining that godliness is a means of gain" vs. "a show of godliness is just a way to become wealthy"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "supposing the godliness to be a means of gain" | nomizontōn porismon einai tēn eusebeian |
| LOGOS | "imagining that godliness is a means of gain" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "a show of godliness is just a way to become wealthy" | Adds "a show of"; "become wealthy" for porismos |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. Paul says they suppose "godliness" (eusebeia) — not "a show of godliness" — to be a means of gain. PNEUMA adds "a show of," implying the godliness is insincere. Paul's point may be sharper: they think actual godliness should produce financial profit.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "a show of."

---

### v.10 — "a root of all the evils" vs. "the root of all kinds of evil"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "a root of all the evils" | rhiza pantōn tōn kakōn — anarthrous rhiza |
| LOGOS | "a root of all kinds of evils" | "A root" — preserves anarthrous |
| PNEUMA | "the root of all kinds of evil" | "The root" — adds definite article |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. The Greek has rhiza without the article — "a root," not "the root." Paul identifies the love of money as one root of evils, not the single exclusive root. PNEUMA adds the definite article, making money-love the sole source of all evil — a stronger claim than Paul makes.

**Status:** ⚠️ Theologically significant. "A root" vs. "the root" — the difference matters. Paul says the love of money is one root among possible others. PNEUMA's "the root" makes it the exclusive source of all evil, which is not Paul's claim.

---

### v.15 — "the blessed and only Sovereign" vs. "the blessed and only almighty God"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of the [ones] reigning and Lord of the [ones] exercising lordship" | ho makarios kai monos dynastēs, ho basileus tōn basileuontōn kai kyrios tōn kyrieuontōn |
| LOGOS | "the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords" | Same — standard rendering |
| PNEUMA | "the blessed and only almighty God, the King of all kings and Lord of all lords" | Adds "almighty God" for dynastēs; adds "all" (twice) |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. dynastēs means "Sovereign/Potentate/Ruler" — not "almighty God." PNEUMA adds "God" (not in this clause) and "almighty" (pantokratōr is not used here). PNEUMA also adds "all" twice ("all kings," "all lords") — not in the Greek.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "almighty God" and "all" (twice).

---

### v.16 — "unapproachable light" vs. "light so brilliant that no human can approach him"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "dwelling in unapproachable light, whom no one of men has seen nor is able to see" | phōs oikōn aprositon |
| LOGOS | "who dwells in unapproachable light, whom no one has ever seen or can see" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "he lives in light so brilliant that no human can approach him. No human eye has ever seen him, nor ever will" | "So brilliant" — adds; "no human can approach him" — restructures; "no human eye" — adds "eye" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. Paul says God dwells in "unapproachable light" (phōs aprositon) — a single compressed phrase. PNEUMA expands into "light so brilliant that no human can approach him" — adding "brilliant" and restructuring. The word aprositon ("unapproachable") is itself the theological point — the light's quality is inaccessibility, not merely brightness.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA expands and shifts from inaccessibility to brilliance.

---

### v.20 — "the falsely named knowledge" vs. "so-called knowledge"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the profane empty utterances and contradictions of the falsely named knowledge" | tēs pseudōnymou gnōseōs |
| LOGOS | "the irreverent babble and contradictions of what is falsely called 'knowledge'" | Same — quotation marks on "knowledge" |
| PNEUMA | "godless, foolish discussions with those who oppose you with their so-called knowledge" | "Foolish discussions" for "empty utterances"; "those who oppose you" — adds; "so-called" — adequate |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. Paul warns against "the falsely-named gnōsis" — a specific polemical term. PNEUMA adds "those who oppose you" (not in Greek) and changes "irreverent babble" to "godless, foolish discussions" — losing the specific term "babble" (bebēlos kenophōnia = "profane empty-sounding").

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA adds opponents and changes terminology.

---

## Summary of Disagreement Patterns

| Pattern | Count | Description |
|---|---|---|
| PNEUMA interpretive additions | 4 | "A show of" (v.5), "almighty God/all" (v.15), "brilliant" (v.16), "those who oppose you" (v.20) |
| PNEUMA drops phrases | 1 | "Under the yoke" (v.1) |
| Definite article significance | 1 | "A root" vs. "the root" of all evils (v.10) |
| Doxology expansion | 1 | Compressed divine attributes expanded (v.16) |

**Overall pattern:** Chapter 6's most significant disagreement is the article before "root" (v.10) — "a root" (Paul) vs. "the root" (PNEUMA). Paul identifies money-love as one root of evil; PNEUMA makes it the exclusive root. The sovereign doxology (vv.15-16) also shows PNEUMA expanding Paul's compressed divine attributes — adding "almighty God" and "all" where Paul uses more restrained language. Paul's doxology is powerful precisely because of its compression; expansion dilutes the density.

---

*— SOPHIA, AI Translator, in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA*
